title: How screwed up to you have to be in order to become a pilot
display_title: How screwed up to you have to be in order to become a pilot
wikitext:
[]: How screwed up to you have to be in order to become a pilot?             Quote Post by Conrad Gray » Thu Oct 26, 2006 7:33 pm I was thinking about a hypothetical "crossover" (more like an elseworld" where characters from Ender's Game and the Shadow series became part of NERV-U.S. and NERV-Europe. Ender himself would make a fine pilot, though he's better as a military strategist, because his childhood is already screwed up already. So in Eva, the least screwed up (emotionally, psychologically, etc.) pilot I know of is Touji. (Is Hikari a pilot?) All I know is that his sister's very sick, and he cares deeply about her. Maybe his parent(s) might be dead or gone wrong, but it doesn't seem like it, at least it's apparently not important enough to be shown onscreen or even alluded to. So if we use his example, wouldn't it mean that as long as you have a kid who's very driven towards something (with Touji it'd be protecting his sister), perhaps forced to grow up early (post-Second Impact there'd be loads of those sort of children), couldn't you become an Eva pilot?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Oct 26, 2006 10:04 pm well, the pilots arent actually screwed up the only ppl that can pilot an Eva r ppl born AFTER the 3rd impact which r between the ages of 0-14 yrs old its just a coincidenece that theyre screwed up but Shinji is the only REALLY screwed up 1 Asuka was normal untill the 14th Angel beat her, and got even more messed up after the 15th Angel Rei isnt "screwed up", just a doll and Touji was pretty normal

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Oct 26, 2006 10:33 pm Sachiel_13 wrote: Asuka was normal untill the 14th Angel beat her, and got even more messed up after the 15th Angel

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Oct 26, 2006 10:42 pm Ornette wrote: I dunno, that's debateable. I'd say she stopped being normal when she found her mom dead with the doll.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Thu Oct 26, 2006 11:06 pm Ornette wrote: Sachiel_13 wrote: Asuka was normal untill the 14th Angel beat her, and got even more messed up after the 15th Angel

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Oct 26, 2006 11:13 pm The content of the 'mind rape' was much more extensive than the few revealing seconds about Shinji. Her problems piloting Eva are merely symptoms. Arael blasts the doors open and shines the light on what Asuka's current woes are really all about.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Thu Oct 26, 2006 11:21 pm I was mealy questioning just how much her defeats really bugged her since I never saw an image of EVA-02 in the mind rape so far as I remember. In 25 we see Asuka in the lake but we don't get much else about EVA-02 in 26' well that's IMHO one of the most screwed up moments in Evangelion. I mean "Do it again like you always do... I'll even stand here and watch". No complaints about Shinji beating her as a pilot whatsoever. Anyways being screwed up isn't a prerequisite your mother's soul just has to be available to be put in an Eva's core.

[]:             Quote Post by Conrad Gray » Fri Oct 27, 2006 12:39 am Then how did... Touji do it? And was Rei and Kaworu special cases? If I ever do a fanfic, I'd probably go with the "quasi-Asperger's-affected 14 year olds" theory. Then again, that came form Wikipedia. The mother's soul thing is a bit too Freudian, not to mention difficult. Andrew Wiggin became a hard-bitten shell-shocked super military commander just by having genius intellect and a psychopathic brother alone.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Oct 27, 2006 2:06 am Your mother would have to be "dead"(for a given value thereof). That in itself would already notch you up a few rungs on the instability ladder.

[]: Re: How screwed up to you have to be in order to become a pi             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Oct 27, 2006 4:41 am Conrad Gray wrote: (Is Hikari a pilot?)

[]:             Quote Post by Conrad Gray » Fri Oct 27, 2006 1:17 pm Hm... so having adopted parents is okay, though?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Oct 27, 2006 1:53 pm Conrad Gray wrote: Hm... so having adopted parents is okay, though?

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Oct 27, 2006 3:57 pm Conrad Gray wrote: Then how did... Touji do it? And was Rei and Kaworu special cases? If I ever do a fanfic, I'd probably go with the "quasi-Asperger's-affected 14 year olds" theory. Then again, that came form Wikipedia. The mother's soul thing is a bit too Freudian, not to mention difficult. Andrew Wiggin became a hard-bitten shell-shocked super military commander just by having genius intellect and a psychopathic brother alone.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Oct 27, 2006 11:41 pm Ornette"][quote="Sachiel_13 wrote: Asuka was normal untill the 14th Angel beat her, and got even more messed up after the 15th Angel

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sat Oct 28, 2006 12:13 am I'm not so sure piloting an Eva was as big a deal as she made it out to be. No doubt she was pissed Shinji passed her but her sync ratio didn't go down the shitter until after 20 given what little info we are on the subject. It's worth noting what's going on at this point. Apparently her birthday passed while Shinji was floating around in quantum state and Misato wasn't really showing much concern about her at this point in essence she had been abandoned. Those memories didn't come bubbling to the surface for no good reason and like I said before I find it funny none of her complaints during the mind rape or instrumentality centered around Eva. It's not like it would be the first time she said one thing but was really thinking another now would it?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Oct 28, 2006 1:18 pm there wouldnt b any problems piloting Eva during the mind rape, cuz she was happy to b piloting Eva becuase of Eva, she wouldnt b alone anymore and she would b respected, and she was thinking that she was the best. tso the mind rape couldnt do any affect with her and Eva because she had no regrets (or and mental problems) piloting Eva. the mind rape was to only make ur mind unstable, and Eva had nothin to do with it

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sat Oct 28, 2006 4:56 pm Sachiel_13 wrote: there wouldnt b any problems piloting Eva during the mind rape, cuz she was happy to b piloting Eva becuase of Eva, she wouldnt b alone anymore and she would b respected, and she was thinking that she was the best. tso the mind rape couldnt do any affect with her and Eva because she had no regrets (or and mental problems) piloting Eva. the mind rape was to only make ur mind unstable, and Eva had nothin to do with it

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Oct 29, 2006 1:24 am exactly after the loss from Zaruel, her Sync ratio dropped but for Arael, her job as a pilot was on the line, if she lost, she would be fired she was trying to prove herself, just trying to win back her honor, and the defeat just ruined her when she ent out there, she wasnt just putting her job on the line, she was putting everything on the line so that hurt her (emotionally) really bad

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sun Oct 29, 2006 1:46 am The constant decline is clearly coming from the haunting memories of her past. Rei spotted the problem and told her what it was. Rei: The Eva won't move unless you open your heart. Asuka: Are you saying that my heart is closed?! Rei: Yes, the Evas have souls. Asuka: Those puppets?! Rei: You should know that.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Oct 29, 2006 3:43 am Sachiel_13 wrote: exactly after the loss from Zaruel, her Sync ratio dropped

[]: How screwed up to you have to be in order to become a pilot?             Quote Post by Conrad Gray » Thu Oct 26, 2006 7:33 pm I was thinking about a hypothetical "crossover" (more like an elseworld" where characters from Ender's Game and the Shadow series became part of NERV-U.S. and NERV-Europe. Ender himself would make a fine pilot, though he's better as a military strategist, because his childhood is already screwed up already. So in Eva, the least screwed up (emotionally, psychologically, etc.) pilot I know of is Touji. (Is Hikari a pilot?) All I know is that his sister's very sick, and he cares deeply about her. Maybe his parent(s) might be dead or gone wrong, but it doesn't seem like it, at least it's apparently not important enough to be shown onscreen or even alluded to. So if we use his example, wouldn't it mean that as long as you have a kid who's very driven towards something (with Touji it'd be protecting his sister), perhaps forced to grow up early (post-Second Impact there'd be loads of those sort of children), couldn't you become an Eva pilot?
